expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Here's what you've got — it's actually a great set"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Between your notes, spreadsheet, the park-fee PDF, the photo, and your voice memo, you've actually captured the whole trip. It's just scattered — which is exactly what you said. Here's the inventory:"]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"📝 tanzaniamemories.md"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Your running trip notes — key stops, practical tips, things to remember, and a few to-dos. Covers the route, the safari operator details (Richard at the office), and assorted advice you've collected."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"💰 trip-costs.xlsx"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Your cost tracker spreadsheet — every expense broken down by category with amounts and notes. This has the real numbers: flights, safari balance, accommodation, tips, the works."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"🏞️ ngorongoro-conservation-area-tariffs.pdf"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["The official Ngorongoro Conservation Area fee schedule — entry fees, camping fees, vehicle fees, and crucially the validity dates so you know what applies when you're there."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"📸 stone-town-zanzibar.jpg"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["A courtyard shot looking up at one of those classic Stone Town buildings — whitewashed walls, arched windows with wooden shutters, carved dark-wood balconies stacked up. It's your Zanzibar anchor photo."]}]},{"type":"Card","props":{"title":"🎙️ Voice memo — Ngorongoro crater day"},"children":[{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You recorded this the day of the crater visit. Key bits: Emmanuel (not Richard) was your driver-guide — brilliant. You bought two beaded bracelets from a Maasai lady at the rim for Athena Mari, paid roughly 35,000 TZS (around $20–25, you weren't sure). And crucially — Emmanuel mentioned an ",{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["extra ranger tip of ~5,000 TZS cash at the gate"]},", separate from park fees, that nobody told you about beforehand. You wanted to ask Richard about that before the safari balance payment."]}]},{"type":"Divider","props":{},"children":[]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You've got four separate containers — notes, costs, official fees, and a voice memo with a half-remembered expense — plus a photo that lives nowhere organized. If you tried to pull up \"what do I still owe and what's the gate situation\" on your phone at the crater rim you'd be flicking between four apps and hoping."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"What I'd suggest"},"children":["I can turn all of this into a single app you can open on your phone — one place with your itinerary, every cost tracked, the park fees loaded in, the voice-memo facts captured (including that ranger-tip question and the bracelet purchase), and your Stone Town photo right there on the Zanzibar section. Tables for costs and fees, pages to browse, and the loose ends flagged so you don't forget to ask Richard."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Want me to put this together for you?"]}]}

args.query: Summarize each document. For the markdown notes, give the key facts and to-dos. For the PDF, extract every fee amount, category, and the validity dates. Be thorough — this is the source material for building an organized trip record.
List every row of this spreadsheet with its cost, category, and any notes. Do not summarize, do not aggregate — I need every individual row.
